hooks.name: scan-secrets
hooks.description: Local-first secret detection using gitleaks before any file reaches the model. Blocks context with detected secrets to prevent data leakage.

action.command:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '🔍 Scanning for secrets using gitleaks...'

if ! command -v gitleaks &> /dev/null; then
  echo '❌ ERROR: gitleaks not found'
  echo ''
  echo 'Install gitleaks:'
  echo '  macOS:   brew install gitleaks'
  echo '  Linux:   Download from https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases'
  echo '  Windows: choco install gitleaks'
  exit 1
fi

GITLEAKS_CONFIG="${GITLEAKS_CONFIG:-.gitleaks.toml}"
GITLEAKS_CMD="gitleaks detect --source=. --verbose --redact"

if [ -f "$GITLEAKS_CONFIG" ]; then
  GITLEAKS_CMD="$GITLEAKS_CMD --config $GITLEAKS_CONFIG"
  echo "Using custom config: $GITLEAKS_CONFIG"
fi

if $GITLEAKS_CMD; then
  echo '✅ No secrets detected - safe to proceed'
  exit 0
else
  EXIT_CODE=$?
  echo ''
  echo '❌ SECRETS DETECTED - Context blocked for security'
  echo ''
  echo 'How to fix:'
  echo '  1. Remove secrets from code'
  echo '  2. Use environment variables or secret management (AWS Secrets Manager)'
  echo '  3. For false positives: add # gitleaks:allow comment to the line'
  echo ''
  echo 'Example:'
  echo '  const EXAMPLE_KEY = "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE" // gitleaks:allow'
  exit $EXIT_CODE
fi

metadata.purpose: Local secret scanning before model access
requirements: 3.1: Local secret scanning with gitleaks before model access

customization.paths: Adjust matcher pattern to match your repository structure
customization.config: Create .gitleaks.toml in repository root for custom rules
customization.whitelist: Use inline comments: # gitleaks:allow for false positives

impact.why_it_matters: File content never leaves the machine. Scan runs locally using gitleaks. If secrets detected, blocks file from being included in model context - defense before the network layer.
detects: API tokens (OpenAI sk-*, GitHub ghp_*, Stripe sk_live_*)

integration: Combine with validate-iam.json for IAM policy validation
Use with pre-send-scan.json for final validation
Part of defense-in-depth: file-level + context-level + network-level
